articleSummary: This technical tutorial provides a Bash-based framework for automating Linux server performance testing using the `sysbench` utility. The author’s core thesis is that performance benchmarking should be simplified through reusable scripts to avoid memorizing complex command-line arguments. Intended for system administrators and DevOps engineers, the guide details how to programmatically assess CPU prime number calculation and disk I/O (sequential and random) while logging results to a central directory. The tone is practical and instructional, framing the scripts as a "library" of shortcuts that can be sourced or run as batch jobs to ensure consistent testing across different hardware environments.
